notes_md:
**Tốn** means “to cost” or “to take up (money, time, effort).” It’s used when something requires you to spend or use resources.

It usually goes with money, time, or effort: *tốn tiền, tốn thời gian, tốn công sức*. You can also use it with “bao nhiêu” to ask how much something costs.


Close word: **tốn kém** (more negative, “costly/expensive” in a painful way). Opposite idea: **tiết kiệm** (to save, economical).
notes:
Tốn means “to cost” or “to take up (money, time, effort).” It’s used when something requires you to spend or use resources.

It usually goes with money, time, or effort: tốn tiền, tốn thời gian, tốn công sức. You can also use it with “bao nhiêu” to ask how much something costs.

Close word: tốn kém (more negative, “costly/expensive” in a painful way). Opposite idea: tiết kiệm (to save, economical).
exampleSentences: Đi lại bằng taxi ở thành phố này rất tốn tiền.
Mua đồ ăn ở nhà hàng sang trọng sẽ tốn nhiều hơn ăn ở nhà.
Em nghĩ mình nên tiết kiệm để không tốn quá nhiều mỗi tháng.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Taking a taxi in this city will cost money.
Buying food at a fancy restaurant will cost more than eating at home.
I think I should save so I do not cost too much each month.

translation.en: This takes a lot of time.

definitions.vi: Tốn là tiêu tốn hoặc chiếm dụng (thời gian, tiền bạc, tài nguyên).
definitions.en: To cost or to take up (time, money, resources).

example.vi: Việc này tốn nhiều thời gian.
example.en: This task takes a lot of time.
